name: Home-Made Ginger Beer
recipeInstructions:
This homemade ginger beer is a favorite party drink in a Welsh household for both adults and children. Use eight pint bottles or four quart bottles with strong material and firm metal-sprung caps to contain the effervescent liquid, which can be more explosive than champagne. It makes 1 gallon.
1. Roughly crush the ginger root.
2. Wash and slice the lemons into thick rings.
3. Combine the ginger, lemon slices, cream of tartar, and sugar in a large bowl.
4. Pour boiling water over the mixture.
5. Stir until well combined and allow to cool to blood temperature (approximately 70°F/21°C).
6. Add the yeast and stir gently.
7. Allow to ferment for 24 hours. Remove any remaining yeast with a fine-mesh strainer or cheesecloth.
8. Bottle the liquid into prepared containers, leaving about an inch of headspace.
9. Store in a cool place for three days before consuming.
